meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no testing knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no testing knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Advanced Checklist for Students
content.seo_description: Master AI-driven debugging to overcome testing gaps. A professional checklist for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to build robust software.
content.answer_block: Stop fearing the 'no testing' void. By integrating Antigravity for automated test generation and Claude Code for iterative debugging, you transform raw, untested code into production-grade logic. This workflow bridges the gap between student prototypes and professional software, ensuring your portfolio projects demonstrate reliability, scalability, and technical architectural maturity.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks architecting your first major SaaS project, but as the codebase swells, a cold realization sets in: your logic is untethered. Every push feels like a gamble, and the absence of a testing suite is the invisible wall between your current build and a professional-grade deployment. The anxiety of silent, cascading failures is the mark of a developer ready to level up.

Relief isn't found in manual trial-and-error, but in leveraging AI as your automated QA department. By treating debugging as a structured engineering process rather than a guessing game, you shift from a 'code-and-pray' mindset to a rigorous, AI-augmented workflow. It is time to turn your technical debt into a masterclass in software resilience.

steps.title: Analyze the Failure Surface
steps.description: Identify the high-risk modules where bugs are most likely to hide. Use AI to map out your application's state transitions and potential edge cases before writing a single test.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase for state-management vulnerabilities and identify the top three modules requiring immediate unit test coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Identify the most fragile functions in this file and explain why they are prone to runtime errors.'

steps.title: Generate Scaffolded Test Suites
steps.description: Don't write boilerplate. Use Antigravity to generate high-coverage test scaffolds based on your existing function signatures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive Jest test suite for the selected module, including mock implementations for external API dependencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file for index.ts covering edge cases for null inputs and array boundaries.'

steps.title: Simulate Failure States
steps.description: Force your application to break. Use AI to inject 'chaos' into your data flows to see how your error handling (or lack thereof) behaves.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a series of negative test cases that deliberately provide malformed JSON inputs to the core processing engine.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write a test that mocks a network timeout and verifies the application handles the exception gracefully.'

steps.title: Iterative Repair Loop
steps.description: Use Claude Code to apply fixes based on test output. Instead of fixing bugs manually, feed the test failure logs directly into the AI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current test failure logs. Suggest the minimal code change to satisfy the failing test while preserving existing logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the latest test failure output and refactor the function to handle the error correctly.'

steps.title: Regression Validation
steps.description: Ensure your fixes didn't break secondary features. Run a full regression sweep using your new automated test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the full test suite and confirm that all previous and current tests pass with a coverage threshold of at least 85%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run all tests in the project and report any regressions. If any fail, provide a diff to fix them.'

pro_tips: Always use 'chain-of-thought' prompting when debugging; ask the AI to explain the logic flow before suggesting a code fix.
Treat your test suite as documentation; if a test is hard to write, your code is likely too complex and needs refactoring.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to maintain context across long-running debugging sessions to avoid repetitive explanations.
Implement 'snapshot testing' for UI components to catch visual regressions that logic-based tests might miss.
Never accept the first AI suggestion; ask it to provide two alternative implementations and choose the one with the lowest cyclomatic complexity.
content.next_steps: Once your tests pass, integrate this workflow into your GitHub Actions pipeline to run on every pull request, effectively automating your QA process forever.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an engineer who builds with confidence and precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
